rulings.Case Title: VICTOR SIENZE et al VS. CLARKE CONSTRUCTION CO. et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, AUGUST 17, 2007, LINE 1. DEFENDANT REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. THERE IS NO STATUTORY AUTHORITY FOR PLAINTIFF'S NEGLIGENCE CLAIM. GOVERNMENT CODE SECTION 815 (A). CREASON V DEPT. OF HEALTH SERVICES (1998) 18 CAL 4TH 623, 630-31. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO PRESENT FACTS GIVING RISE TO A SPECIAL RELATIONSHIP WITHIN THE MEANING OF WILLIAMS V STATE (1983) 34 CAL 3D 18, 27-28. PLAINTIFF'S PREMISES LIABILITY CLAIM FAILS BECAUSE PLAINTIFF HAS NOT IDENTIFIED A CONDITION OF THE PROPERTY THAT CONTRIBUTED TO PLAINTIFF'S INJURIES. =(302/PJM/AP)

rulings.Case Title: CARL RANSDELL VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, AUGUST 17, 2007, LINE 2. DEFENDANT EATON ELECTRICAL INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. DECEDENT'S DEPOSITION TESTIMONY ESTABLISHES THAT HE WAS EXPOSED TO PLAINTIFF'S PRODUCTS UNDER CIRCUMSTANCES THAT GIVE RISE TO THE INFERENCE THAT DECEDENT WAS EXPOSED TO ASBESTOS AS A CONSEQUENCE OF ELECTRICIANS CUTTING, FILING AND DRILLING THE PRODUCTS. JOEL COHEN'S DECLARATION DOES NOT REBUT DECEDENT'S TESTIMONY. =(302/PJM/AP)

rulings.Case Title: CARL STANGLIN et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, AUGUST 17, 2007, LINE 3. DEFENDANT SEPCO CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT: Hearing required. It appears that Defendant has shifted the burden of proof. It is not clear what evidence Plaintiff is offering to establish that the packing material referred to by Trowbridge contained asbestos. Should the Trowbridge Declaration be struck in light of his admission that parts of it are not true? The Templin Declaration fails to provide a factual basis for the opinion that the products contained asbestos. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: CARL STANGLIN et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, AUGUST 17, 2007, LINE 3. DEFENDANT FOSECO, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT: Defendant has shifted the burden of proof. Although Plaintiff's supplemental interrogatories identify witnesses, the Declaration of Ms. Foster demonstrates the Defendant made a good faith effort to depose any witness with actual knowledge and was unable to do so because Plaintiff lacked the requisite contact information. Although the Trowbridge Declaration confirms that decedent was in proximity to Defendant's products, it is unclear from the record what evidence there is that the products contained asbestos. Paragraph 11 of the Templin Declaration fails to provide a basis for his opinion that any of the products referred to by Trowbrdge contain asbestos, as such it is not admissible for that purpose. The Money Declaration likewise fails to identify what Foseco records are available and so it fails to establish that no asbestos products were sold to the Gardner Foundry. Based on the evidence presented, the motion is granted because Plaintiff has not demonstrated that Plaintiff possesses evidence that the products described by Trowbridge contain asbestos. =(302/PJM/PB)
